Higher = more left = safer
Hover each bar for reset time

security.desc: Login-failure lockout thresholds and durations — adjustable or off

oauthLogin.title: Quick Login (Recommended)
oauthLogin.desc: Click a button to open Windsurf's official sign-in page, log in with Google/GitHub, then paste the returned token to add the account

emailLogin.title: Email & Password Login
emailLogin.desc: For accounts registered with email+password only. Third-party login please use buttons above. Supports single login or batch import in "[proxy] email password" format.

loginProxy.title: Login Proxy (Optional)
loginProxy.desc: Specify a proxy for this login session; leave empty to use global proxy. When proxy takes effect, subsequent chat requests will also egress through this proxy.

loginHistory.title: Login History
loginHistory.desc: Local record of last 50 login operations

addAccount.title: Add Account
addAccount.desc: Supports both API Key and Auth Token methods
addAccount.hint: Recommended: Use Token method. Visit {{link}} after logging in, copy the Token and paste below. Works with all login methods (Email/Google/GitHub).

smartImport.title: Smart Import
smartImport.desc: Paste to auto-detect, batch supported. Recognizes Session Token / Auth1 / Firebase Refresh / email-password / labeled pairs, mixable.
smartImport.placeholder: devin-session-token$...  or  auth1_...  or  email----password  — one per line, mixable
smartImport.empty: Paste some tokens or accounts first

accountList.title: Account List
accountList.desc: Click probe button to update capability info for a specific account

modelPolicy.title: Access Policy
modelPolicy.desc: Choose "Allow All" for no restrictions; "Allowlist" only permits listed models; "Blocklist" blocks listed models

modelList.title: Model List
modelList.descAllow: Only selected models are allowed; unselected models return 403 error
modelList.descBlock: Selected models will be blocked; unselected models work normally

globalProxy.title: Global Proxy
globalProxy.desc: Accounts without dedicated proxy will use this configuration

accountProxy.title: Per-Account Proxy
accountProxy.desc: Set dedicated proxy for specific accounts; each dedicated proxy spawns an independent LS instance

identityInjection.title: Model Identity Injection
identityInjection.desc: When enabled, injects a system prompt at the start of each request, overriding Cascade's built-in Windsurf identity; each provider's template below can be customized and takes effect immediately after saving.

cascadeReuse.title: Cascade Conversation Reuse
cascadeReuse.desc: Reuse the same cascade_id across multi-turn conversations, only sending the latest user message to Windsurf. Let the server maintain context cache. Significantly reduces TTFB and upload volume on cache hits; automatically falls back to new session on cache miss or account/LS change. Requires client to retain full history and append in order (e.g., new-api, OpenWebUI). Note: this toggle only governs cascade_id reuse across requests — it does NOT control whether Cascade is used. Tool-emulated requests (Claude Code / Cline / Cursor) auto-skip reuse because each turn's content changes, regardless of this setting.
cascadeReuse.hint: Enabled by default. When disabled, Cascade is still used but a fresh session is created per turn. Takes effect immediately on current conversation pool.

identityPrompt.title: Model Identity Prompt
identityPrompt.desc: Inject model identity system prompt at the start of each request.
identityPrompt.hint: Enabled by default; templates below won't work when disabled.
identityPrompt.templateHint: Each provider's template is editable. {{model}} placeholder will be replaced with the requested model name.

systemPrompts.title: System Prompts
systemPrompts.desc: Tool injection, conversation mode and other built-in prompt templates. Changes take effect immediately; click "Reset" to restore defaults.

credentials.title: Credentials
credentials.desc: Rotate API_KEY and the dashboard password at runtime. Saved into runtime-config.json and applied immediately — no container restart or .env reload needed. Next request must use the new value; the current session will be asked to re-login.

stickyByUser.title: Sticky session per-user binding
stickyByUser.desc: When enabled, the same user is always bound to the same upstream account regardless of model. When off, binding uses (user, model) pairs — different models may land on different accounts (useful when accounts have model-specific restrictions). Requires STICKY_SESSION_ENABLED=1.
stickyByUser.hint: Off by default. When on, user_a's opus/haiku/gemini all share a single binding.

stickyNoFallback.title: Sticky session no-fallback
stickyNoFallback.desc: When enabled, if the account bound by sticky session hits a rate limit, error, or model unavailability, the proxy will NOT fall back to another account in the pool — the error is returned directly to the client. This ensures each user strictly consumes only their bound account's quota. Requires STICKY_SESSION_ENABLED=1.
stickyNoFallback.hint: Off by default. When on, a bound account failure won't consume other accounts' quota.

nativeToolCall.title: Native tool_call
nativeToolCall.desc: When enabled, tool definitions ride the native protobuf wire (calibrated #10 ToolDef + #6 ChatToolCall) instead of prompt emulation (<tool_call> text markup). The native path is more stable for weak models (fable) with many tools and isn't subject to the prompt-emulation empty-reply limit. When off, falls back to prompt emulation (the current stable default).
nativeToolCall.hint: Off by default. Before enabling, send a tools-carrying request to a paid account to verify the round-trip.

preflight.title: Pre-flight quota probe
preflight.desc: Before each chat, probe server.codeium.com for remaining quota so requests to exhausted accounts fail fast instead of burning an upstream 429. Costs one extra round-trip per request.
preflight.hint: Off by default. Adds latency; enable when accounts frequently run dry.

droughtRestrict.title: Block premium models during drought
droughtRestrict.desc: When every active account is below 5% weekly quota, premium model requests (claude-opus / claude-sonnet etc.) are refused with a clean 503 instead of burning the last bit of quota on an upstream 429. Free-tier models (gemini-2.5-flash etc.) still go through. Disable to forward premium requests anyway and accept the upstream rate-limit.
droughtRestrict.hint: Default ON. Drought state is auto-detected from the account pool (GET /dashboard/api/drought for live state).
droughtRestrict.thresholdHint: Accounts below this weekly-quota % count toward drought

skin.title: Interface Style
skin.desc: Dashboard appearance. Modern is the default; sketch is an experimental simplified layout. The page refreshes after switching.

lsPool.sectionDesc: {{count}} Language Server instances · one process per dedicated proxy

modelStats.desc: Aggregated by model: requests, success count, error count, success rate, avg duration and p50/p95 latency percentiles

accountStats.desc: Request volume and success rate per account ID (first 8 chars)

requestChart.title: Request Volume Time Series
requestChart.subtitle: Switch chart type and time range · hover for exact values

modelPie.title: Model Request Distribution
modelPie.desc: Share of requests for top 8 models

emailOtpLogin.title: Email Code Login
emailOtpLogin.desc: No password needed — register a new account via an email verification code. Requires completing the Cloudflare human check.

poolHealth.title: Account Pool Health
poolHealth.desc: Worst-first summary of per-account health signals

connectMetrics.title: Connection Self-Healing
connectMetrics.desc: Live recovery counters — relogin, failover, throttling, dead tokens

overviewTrend.desc: Requests and errors per hour, from local telemetry

batchInput.label: Batch Import
batchInput.hint: One account per line. Supports: email password, email----password, email|password, email,password, email:password. JSON file upload is also supported. Optional proxy format: [proxy] email password.

field.storeCredential: Encrypt & save password for auto-renewal when the session expires
field.storeCredentialHint: Requires DEVIN_CONNECT_CRED_KEY. On a public bind also requires DEVIN_CONNECT_ALLOW_REMOTE_CRED_STORE=1. The password is AES-256-GCM encrypted, never logged.

otpCode.label: OTP Code
otpCode.hint: Get the 6-digit code from your email

login.title: Control Panel Login
login.subtitle: Please enter the admin password to continue
login.lockedDesc: Server bound to public host without DASHBOARD_PASSWORD. v2.0.55 fail-closed: API_KEY no longer doubles as the dashboard password.
login.locked: Backend has no password configured — set DASHBOARD_PASSWORD or API_KEY env and restart

cta.title: Want to join this list?
cta.desc: Open an issue or pull request on GitHub — bug fixes, new models, UI improvements, or security findings all get recognised here.

cascadeReuse.title: Enable Cascade Conversation Reuse
cascadeReuse.desc: Disabled by default. When enabled, takes effect immediately on current conversation pool.
cascadeReuse.hint: Disabled by default. When enabled, takes effect immediately on current conversation pool.

identityPrompt.title: Enable Model Identity Injection
identityPrompt.desc: Enabled by default; templates below won't work when disabled.

experimental.templateHint: Each provider's template is editable. {{model}} placeholder will be replaced with the requested model name (e.g., claude-opus-4.6).

drought.title: Quota drought:
drought.body: Every active account is below the weekly quota threshold — add accounts or wait for reset
drought.detail: min weekly={{weekly}}% · min daily={{daily}}% · {{known}}/{{active}} accounts known

help.devinCliMode: Devin CLI transport. print: one-shot mode — send the prompt, read the printed reply; no tool calls or streaming. acp (Agent Client Protocol): a persistent two-way session with native tool calls, image/vision input, and streaming. Use acp for tool-using or multimodal models; print is lighter for plain chat.
help.devinConnect: Direct cloud HTTP backend. When on, requests go straight to Devin's cloud API over HTTP instead of driving a local Devin CLI process. Deploy-time switch — off means native CLI tools are unavailable.
help.devinOnly: Route every request through the Devin CLI backend only, disabling all other upstream providers. Use when you want a single, predictable backend.
help.allowClientTools: Allow client-side tool calls and image/vision input to reach the model (DEVIN_CLI_ALLOW_CLIENT_TOOLS). Required for native tool use and image understanding; leave off for text-only chat.
help.loginHostFallback: If the primary login host fails, fall back to Devin's direct login endpoint. Improves login success on flaky networks.
help.autoRelogin: Automatically re-authenticate an account when its session dies mid-request, instead of failing the request.
help.remoteCredStore: Whether the server may store account passwords for remote self-healing login. A security boundary — read-only here.
help.credStoreEnabled: Whether a credential encryption key (CRED_KEY) is configured, enabling encrypted at-rest storage of stored credentials.
help.cascadeReuse: Cascade reuse keeps one cascade_id across a multi-turn chat and sends only your latest message, so the server holds context server-side. Hits cut TTFB and upload size; a miss or an account/LS change silently falls back to a fresh session.
help.drought: Drought mode: auto-detected when every active account's weekly quota drops below the threshold. While active, premium models (opus/sonnet) can be blocked with a 503 to avoid burning the last quota on upstream rate-limits; free-tier models stay available.
help.stickySession: Sticky session pins a user to one upstream account so repeat requests reuse the same quota and cache. Requires STICKY_SESSION_ENABLED=1. Bind-by-user ignores the model dimension; no-fallback refuses to switch accounts on error.
help.tier: Account tier drives which models are allowed, read from Windsurf's per-account allowed-models config. Free accounts often still include GLM/SWE/Kimi. Set manually to pro when a trial mis-detects to force-unlock every model.
help.circuitBreaker: The circuit breaker disables an account after repeated errors within a window, then re-enables it with exponential backoff. Thresholds are hot-editable; blank fields fall back to the env var / default.
help.tokenCache: Token cache read ratio: share of prompt tokens served from the upstream context cache instead of re-uploaded. Higher is cheaper and faster; reuse depends on cascade reuse and stable sessions.
help.promptCredits: Prompt credits are your plan's included monthly quota for prompt-model requests, shown as remaining / limit. Once exhausted, requests either stop or spill to on-demand (overage) depending on the plan.
help.flowCredits: Flow credits (a.k.a. flex credits) are the included monthly quota for agentic/flow actions, separate from prompt credits, shown as remaining / limit.
help.overage: On-demand (overage) balance: usage-based spend billed beyond your included quota, in USD. Accrues only after included credits run out.
help.weeklyQuota: Weekly quota %: portion of the account's rolling weekly allowance consumed. Low values across all accounts trigger drought mode.
help.poolHealthView: Glow grid: one breathing core per account, tinted by health — cores catch fire when RPM saturates and flash when a request flows through. Status bars: one dense row per account showing RPM / in-flight / success·fail / last-used. Hover any account for details.
help.trendRange: Switch the request-trend window: 24 hours (hourly), 7 days or 30 days (aggregated per day). Data is local telemetry, no upstream call.

credentials.apiKeyPlaceholder: New API_KEY (≥ 8 chars; leave blank to clear runtime override)
credentials.dashboardPwPlaceholder: New dashboard password (≥ 8 chars; leave blank to clear runtime override)
credentials.apiKeyHint: After saving, every chat client must use the new KEY; the old KEY stops working immediately.
credentials.dashboardPwHint: Stored as a scrypt hash; the current session will be signed out and you'll need to log in again with the new password.
credentials.bruteForceHint: Brute-force guard: same IP gets locked for 30 minutes after 5 failed dashboard logins (since v2.0.56).
credentials.confirmSave: After saving, the old value stops working immediately. Continue?
credentials.confirmClear: Clear the runtime override and fall back to the .env value?

confirm.updateLsDesc: Download the latest language_server from the WindsurfAPI / Windsurf desktop LS / Exafunction GitHub release and restart the LS pool. In-flight requests will fail once.
confirm.clearPoolDesc: All active Cascade sessions will be cleared. Continue?
confirm.clearProxyDesc: All outbound requests will go direct until a proxy is set again. Continue?
confirm.switchSkinDesc: The dashboard will reload to apply the new skin. Continue?
confirm.expToggleDesc: This changes live routing behavior immediately. Continue?

blockedModels.title: Edit Available Models — {{email}}
blockedModels.hint: Checked = available; Uncheck = disabled for this account

deleteAccount.title: Delete Account
deleteAccount.desc: This action cannot be undone. Delete this account?

overrideTier.title: Manually Set Account Tier
overrideTier.desc: Current: {{current}}. The proxy routes by Windsurf's upstream cascade_allowed_models_config (per-account — free accounts often include GLM / SWE / Kimi etc.). When the Pro trial mis-detects (issue #8), manually set to pro to force-unlock every model.

applyUpdate.title: Update & Restart
applyUpdate.desc: Will execute git pull and restart the service process. Dashboard will be briefly unavailable during restart (about 5-10 seconds). Continue?

The following files have been modified but not committed:

Continuing will overwrite local changes with remote version. For non-git deployments (SFTP / zip), click force overwrite.

oauth.step2Desc: After signing in on the new tab, the page shows a token string — copy it here and click Add.
oauth.openManually: Sign-in page didn't open? Click here

error.ERR_UNCOMMITTED_CHANGES: Working directory has uncommitted changes. Force overwrite?

error.ERR_PROXY_PRIVATE_IP: Proxy cannot point to private/local address
error.ERR_TLS_TUNNEL_ERROR: TLS tunnel established but returned abnormal content

error.ERR_NO_PASSWORD_SET: No password set for this account. Use Google/GitHub OAuth or visit windsurf.com/show-auth-token to copy Auth Token

error.ERR_TOKEN_FETCH_FAILED: Failed to fetch one-time auth token
error.ERR_LOCAL_IMPORT_LOOPBACK_ONLY: Local import only available from 127.0.0.1 — open the dashboard from the host machine itself, not via a public domain
error.ERR_LOCAL_IMPORT_FAILED: Failed to scan local Windsurf credentials

error.ERR_OAUTH_UPSTREAM: The login provider returned an error. Please retry the sign-in.

settings.oauthSkipChooser: Skip the "open / copy" chooser popup on Google/GitHub login — open the login page directly
settings.emailLockThreshold.hint: Lock an email after this many failed logins (0 = off)
settings.emailLockMinutes.hint: How long a locked email stays locked
settings.ipLockThreshold.hint: Ban an IP after this many failed dashboard logins (0 = off)
settings.ipLockMinutes.hint: How long a banned IP stays banned
settings.lockZeroHint: Set a threshold to 0 to disable that lockout

env.devinOnly: DEVIN_ONLY (Devin CLI as the only backend)
env.hint: The routing switches above can be flipped live without a redeploy. A switch showing "env" is following its environment variable / default; flip it to set an override, or reset it to fall back again. The two credential-store rows are a security boundary and stay read-only.
env.srcEnvHint: Following the environment variable / default (no override set)
env.confirmRouting: This switch affects the backend routing of all subsequent requests; in-flight sessions may be disrupted. Continue?

breaker.errorStreakThreshold.hint: Errors within the window before the account is disabled (default 3)
breaker.errorWindowMin.hint: Errors older than this reset the streak (default 30)
breaker.internalErrorThreshold.hint: Consecutive upstream internal errors before a quarantine (default 2)
breaker.internalQuarantineMin.hint: How long a quarantined account stays out (default 2)
breaker.errorRecoveryMin.hint: How long an error'd account waits before a half-open retry (default 15)
breaker.baseMin.hint: Base cooldown for the exponential ladder (default = recovery TTL)
breaker.factor.hint: Cooldown multiplier per repeat episode (default 1.5, must be >1)
breaker.maxMin.hint: Hard ceiling on the backoff cooldown (default 60)
breaker.streakStart.hint: Episode count at which escalation begins (default 2)
breaker.graceMin.hint: New accounts are exempt from escalation this long; 0 disables (default 10)
breaker.retroHint: Lowering a threshold takes effect on the next error. errorCount accumulates persistently, so an account already near the limit may trip immediately after you lower it.
